name: Academic Writing Improvement (EIGCB Model)
description: Improves academic writing quality using the EIGCB model (Evidence-Interpretation-Gap-Connection-Boundary). Detects generic, repetitive, over-polished, unsupported, or descriptive writing and rewrites it as critical, evidence-led, researcher-owned academic prose.
ethical_boundary: Do not fabricate sources, statistics, interviews, or validation. Do not remove valid citations. Do not overstate what the evidence proves. Originality comes from synthesis, critique, and interpretation — not from removing source dependence.

rules.description: Check if major argument paragraphs follow EIGCB structure: Evidence → Interpretation → Gap → Connection → Boundary. Missing elements reduce quality score.
Flag sections that summarise one author at a time without synthesis, comparison, or researcher interpretation. Literature reviews especially prone.
Detect the same concept defined or explained multiple times across chapters. Strong frameworks should be defined once and applied/referenced thereafter.
Check if chapters open with a relatable scene-setter (named person, specific situation, tension). Missing story openers reduce engagement score.
Measure density of researcher voice markers per 1000 words. Target: 3-8 markers. Too few = generic. Too many = repetitive.
Flag claims that are stronger than the evidence supporting them. Every strong claim needs a citation, limitation, or boundary statement.
Check if long sections use the tension-maintenance rhythm: State → Show → Complicate → Connect every 500-700 words.
Check whether citations are followed by researcher interpretation instead of stacked summaries. Strong originality needs source use plus authorial judgment.
Only rewrite chapters or sections approved by the user. Preserve safe sections and avoid whole-document flattening.

word_lists.word_or_phrase: AI is important in modern organisations
word_lists.replacement: specify what AI does in this exact context
name the specific people or roles

word_lists.word_or_phrase: My interpretation of the evidence is
A limitation of this interpretation is

literature_synthesis.template:
Rewrite this literature review passage as genuine synthesis (not author-by-author summary).

Synthesis structure:
1. What the literature agrees on
2. Where key sources diverge or disagree
3. What gap or tension remains
4. How the researcher's framework responds
5. Cite all sources grouped by claim

Do not summarise one author at a time.
Do not remove any citations.

Passage:
{text}
